summary: Exact-match work in the Melee camera translation unit. The PR changed only src/melee/cm/camera.c, reported 3 newly exact functions, and improved 3 additional unmatched camera functions with no reported broken matches, fuzzy regressions, or metric regressions.

key_files.role: Only changed file; camera matching work covering Camera_8002A0C0, Camera_8002F9E4, Camera_8002FC7C, and improvements in Camera_8002CDDC, Camera_80029CF8, and Camera_800311EC.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl issue_comment: new matches and improvements all in main/melee/cm/camera

what_changed.text: Added a static inline helper get_pitch_angle for part of Camera_80029CF8, moving pitch-angle setup into a helper that takes CameraBounds, a Vec3 scroll offset pointer, and an output f32 pointer.

Renamed many generated-looking temporaries in camera math to role names such as pitch_blend, pan_angle, vert_frustum_dist, horiz_frustum_dist, zoom_depth, horiz_offset, vert_offset, viewport_x_scale, viewport_y_scale, half_view_height, input_x, input_y, and depth_ratio.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Camera_80029CF8 temporary variables renamed and reordered
raw_diff: Camera_8002A0C0 var_f*/temp_f* locals replaced with role names

what_changed.text: In Camera_8002A0C0, introduced a local struct CameraStaticData and cast cm_803BCB18 through it so aspect and viewport are accessed via data->desc rather than directly through cm_803BCB64 for that code path.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: struct CameraStaticData with CameraModeCallbacks, HSD_WObjDesc, HSD_CameraDescPerspective desc
raw_diff: viewport_x_scale and viewport_y_scale use data->desc.aspect and data->desc.viewport

what_changed.text: Adjusted Camera_8002CDDC local ordering, target-interest/target-position pointer usage, fov smoothing expression shape, fallback pointer temporaries for globals->x40/x44, PAD_STACK from 8 to 4, and target distance computation using cam->transform fields.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fallback_path changes PAD_STACK(8) to PAD_STACK(4)
raw_diff: smooth_ptr and fov_ptr added for globals->x44 and globals->x40

what_changed.text: In Camera_8002F9E4 and Camera_8002FC7C, introduced a target_interest pointer and assigned target_position from that pointed target-interest value.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Camera_8002F9E4 adds Vec3* target_interest and uses *target_interest
raw_diff: Camera_8002FC7C adds Vec3* target_interest and uses *target_interest

what_changed.text: In fn_800301D0, renamed s64 var_r* locals to priority-related names prio8_a, prio1_a, prio8_b, and prio1_b while preserving repeated priority assignment logic.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_800301D0 var_r4/var_r0/var_r3/var_r5 renamed to prio8_a/prio1_a/prio8_b/prio1_b

what_changed.text: Reported exact matches for Camera_8002A0C0, Camera_8002F9E4, and Camera_8002FC7C, plus improvements in Camera_8002CDDC, Camera_80029CF8, and Camera_800311EC.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl issue_comment: 📈 3 improvements in unmatched items table

smart_moves.text: Kept the PR scope narrow to one camera translation unit with no header or naming churn reported outside the TU.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: Review risk low; one CM translation unit

smart_moves.text: Used semantic local names to clarify camera math without renaming global/address-style symbols.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: temp_f*/var_f* locals become pan_angle, zoom_depth, horiz_offset, viewport_x_scale, depth_ratio

smart_moves.text: Introduced a narrow local struct for static camera data layout inside Camera_8002A0C0 instead of adding broad global type churn in this PR.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: local struct CameraStaticData declared inside Camera_8002A0C0

smart_moves.text: Included explicit verification notes: isolated changes_all run, clean slice regression report, clean full ship-set regression report, diff check, and review_lint result.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For camera math, extracting a small inline helper can improve match progress while keeping surrounding function structure readable, as shown by get_pitch_angle inside the Camera_80029CF8 work.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: static inline void get_pitch_angle added
text_corpus.jsonl issue_comment: Camera_80029CF8 improved from 98.43% to 98.94%

decomp_lessons.lesson: Role-based local names can coexist with matching-oriented expression reshaping; this PR replaced temp/var locals with camera-domain names while reporting exact matches or improvements.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: temp_f*/var_f* renamed across Camera_80029CF8, Camera_8002A0C0, fn_800301D0
text_corpus.jsonl issue_comment: 3 new matches and 3 improvements

decomp_lessons.lesson: A local temporary struct can be a contained way to express known data layout for matching when a full header/type update is outside PR scope.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: local struct CameraStaticData used to access data->desc.aspect and data->desc.viewport

decomp_lessons.lesson: Pointer temporaries to struct fields can be matching-relevant in MWCC-shaped code; this PR introduced target_interest, tgt_interest2, smooth_ptr, and fov_ptr in specific camera paths.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: target_interest pointer additions in Camera_8002F9E4 and Camera_8002FC7C
raw_diff: tgt_interest2, smooth_ptr, and fov_ptr additions in Camera_8002CDDC
text_corpus.jsonl issue_comment: Camera_8002F9E4 and Camera_8002FC7C became exact matches; Camera_8002CDDC improved

naming_conventions.text: Prefer camera-domain local names over generated temp_r/var_f style once the role is visible: pan_angle, pitch_blend, zoom_depth, horiz_frustum_dist, vert_frustum_dist, horiz_offset, vert_offset, viewport_x_scale, viewport_y_scale, input_x, input_y, depth_ratio.
For bit-derived rendering priority locals, names like prio8_a, prio1_a, prio8_b, and prio1_b document the immediate priority-mask role without claiming broader semantic meaning.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_800301D0 s64 locals renamed from var_r* to prio*

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used a narrow static inline helper with pointer outputs to alter the code shape for Camera_80029CF8 while preserving the surrounding function's stack/local behavior.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: get_pitch_angle takes Vec3* scroll_offset and f32* pitch_angle

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Adjusted PAD_STACK in Camera_8002CDDC fallback_path from 8 to 4, a stack-shape tactic supported here by the PR's reported regression results but not generalizable without local verification.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: Slice regression report 3 new matches, 0 broken matches, 0 fuzzy regressions, 0 metric regressions

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Changed compound assignments into explicit assignment expressions for fov smoothing, likely to match MWCC expression/order shape in Camera_8002CDDC.
Introduced pointer aliases to target_interest and globals fields where direct field access did not produce the desired match shape.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: target_interest pointer in Camera_8002F9E4 and Camera_8002FC7C
raw_diff: smooth_ptr and fov_ptr in Camera_8002CDDC fallback_path

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used the existing MAX macro in Camera_80029CF8 for frustum distance selection rather than preserving a generated branch expression.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Camera matching used a small inline helper plus semantic locals
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2682 improved Camera_80029CF8 by extracting get_pitch_angle and renaming generated temporaries to camera-domain locals; bot report shows Camera_80029CF8 improved from 98.43% to 98.94%. Treat this as local evidence for trying small inline helper extraction and role-based local names in camera math, not as a global rule.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: role-based local names in Camera_80029CF8

accepted_candidate_records.title: Pointer aliases affected exact camera matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2682 introduced target_interest pointer aliases in Camera_8002F9E4 and Camera_8002FC7C; both functions are reported as newly exact. This is narrow evidence that local pointer aliases can be useful for MWCC match shape in this camera TU.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl issue_comment: Camera_8002F9E4 and Camera_8002FC7C new matches

source_update_candidates.title: Document local temporary structs as a contained typed-access tactic
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2682 used a function-local CameraStaticData struct cast for cm_803BCB18 to access desc.aspect and desc.viewport without broader header churn. This may be worth adding as an example under typed-fields-over-pointer-math or matching-tactics-need-evidence: local temporary structs can be acceptable bridge code when narrowly scoped and verified, but should not replace an owning header/type update when the layout is established globally.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: local struct CameraStaticData inside Camera_8002A0C0
source_update_candidates.reason: The current standards already prefer typed fields and require evidence for temporary padded structs, but this PR gives a concrete example of a narrow local struct bridge in matching work.

source_update_candidates.title: Clarify pointer aliases as matching tactics requiring narrow evidence
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2682 introduced field pointer aliases such as target_interest, tgt_interest2, smooth_ptr, and fov_ptr, with exact-match/improvement reports in affected functions. Consider adding pointer alias/lifetime steering to examples covered by matching-tactics-need-evidence.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: pointer aliases in camera functions
text_corpus.jsonl issue_comment: affected functions exact or improved
source_update_candidates.reason: The tactic is supported in this PR, but should remain evidence-gated because pointer aliases can be codegen/lifetime steering.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that get_pitch_angle is an accepted global camera API; it is a static inline helper added within camera.c.
Do not treat PAD_STACK(4) as a preferred replacement for PAD_STACK(8) generally; this is a local stack-shape change supported only in this PR context.
Do not claim Camera_800311EC code-level tactics from the excerpt; the diff is truncated and only the bot improvement report is loaded.
Do not promote the local CameraStaticData layout into a canonical header fact without additional source/type evidence.
